name: institutional_trust_justice
description: Measures citizens' trust and confidence in formal justice institutions — including judges, prosecutor offices, the Supreme Court, and human rights commissions — to uphold rights and deliver justice. Captures perceived legitimacy and effectiveness of the justice system.

name: authoritarian_security_tradeoff
description: Measures the degree to which respondents accept rights violations or coercive state practices — such as torture, post-submission harm by police, and restricting human rights in the name of public security — reflecting a tolerance for authoritarianism when framed as necessary for safety.

name: perceived_discrimination_social_exclusion
description: Measures respondents' perceptions of and attitudes toward discriminatory treatment based on physical appearance, skin color, religion, disability, and sexual orientation, including both personal experiences of rights violations and observed societal discrimination against vulnerable groups.

name: rights_awareness_personal_experience
description: Measures respondents' direct personal experience with human rights violations and labor rights abuses, as well as their sense of protection against authority abuse, capturing the lived dimension of rights rather than abstract attitudes.

name: social_rights_service_quality
description: Measures citizens' evaluations of the state's fulfillment of social and economic rights — including public health services, public education quality, and dignified housing — reflecting perceived adequacy of social rights provision by the government.
